select_when: Observed behavior is easy to name, but the real decision quality depends on identifying the why beneath it.
Stated motivations may be incomplete because image management, politics, or self-deception are part of the situation.
A recommendation must fit what the audience wants to know, feel, and do rather than merely what the analyst finds interesting.
The work would improve if intrinsic and extrinsic drivers were separated instead of being treated as one blended explanation.
avoid_when: The team is over-psychologizing a problem that is better explained by simple constraints, incentives, or capability gaps.
The model is being used to infer hidden motives without enough concrete behavior, context, or contradiction to justify the inference.
output_type: motive structure and action fit frame

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on hidden drivers, intrinsic-versus-extrinsic motive structure, and message-to-motivation fit rather than widening it into generic persuasion rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor motivation-sprawl beyond the raw source's stronger hidden-driver, image-management, and audience-fit surface.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains Wave 1 to Wave 3 only.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans behavioral science, leadership, persuasion, and organizational design.

select_when.note: The source is explicit that the model is strongest when hidden drivers, motive structure, and audience-fit determine whether behavior will change.

avoid_when.note: The anti-pattern section directly warns against over-psychologizing and taking surface declarations as sufficient truth.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on behavior-driver diagnosis problems, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields clearer motive structure and action fit, but the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model is naturally diagnostic of hidden drivers and causal in how motives shape behavior, while the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
